data.description: User reports videos in homepage ClipReel still glitchy / not rendering despite multiple recent fix PRs (#86 #87 #88 #89 #90). Goal: stop the band-aid loop. Read every recent ClipReel commit, identify the underlying root cause (iOS Safari video lifecycle, hydration mismatch from touch detect, aggressive preload, etc.), and either fix it correctly or replace the implementation with something fundamentally simpler. Original goal of ClipReel: vertical scroll-driven wipe through 16 arch-voice clips on desktop (current); some mobile-friendly fallback. Re-evaluate whether the wipe metaphor is even the right thing on mobile, or whether a simpler poster→tap-to-play gallery is correct.
data.title: Fix ClipReel video reliability — investigate why mobile fixes don't stick

data.body: User clarification: ClipReel has bugs on DESKTOP too, not only mobile. The recent fix PRs (#86-#90) all targeted mobile, so the desktop bugs are a separate (or possibly shared root-cause) issue that hasn't been touched. When investigating, repro on desktop FIRST before mobile — desktop is the simpler environment and any bugs there will narrow the suspect list. Things to check on desktop: scroll-driven clip-path wipe behavior across all 16 slides, video preload=metadata loading 16 elements at once, the wipe-line indicator math, video play/pause toggle on click, and what happens at the slide boundaries (z-index stacking, slide 0 riso cover transitioning to slide 1).

When transcoding .mov clips, use a fast ffmpeg preset (-preset veryfast or -preset fast) — slow presets make the batch take far too long. Pair with -movflags +faststart so the moov atom is at the head for instant web playback. Discovered while working on ASMV-90 (click-driven gallery rewrite): all .mov clips in public/videos/software/arch-voice-mcp/ are LFS pointers locally, which is why my mobile reliability fixes never showed any change in this dev environment. Confirmed root cause.
